perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information. Unless instructed otherwise in writing, the parties acknowledge that all information, knowledge, or data relating to the business of Client, including but not limited to: costs and financial information; pricing methods or strategies; existing and potential customers; market studies, plans and strategies; trade secrets, know how, ideas, strategies, inventions, materials, drawings, proprietary information, operational information, and any other non-public information, material or data relating to the past, current and/or future business and operations of Client (including any information, summaries or other compilations extracted from such shared information), shared for review, and in all instances marked as confidential, belongs to and is the sole and exclusive property of Client and shall be deemed confidential ("Confidential Information") and subject to nondisclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: Protected Information. Unless instructed otherwise in writing, the parties acknowledge that all information, knowledge, or data relating to the business of Client, including but not limited to: costs and financial information; pricing methods or strategies; existing and potential customers; market studies, plans and strategies; trade secrets, know how, ideas, strategies, inventions, materials, drawings, proprietary information, operational information, and any other non-public information, material or data relating to the past, current and/or future business and operations of Client (including any information, summaries or other compilations extracted from such shared information), shared for review, and in all instances marked as confidential, belongs to and is the sole and exclusive property of Client and shall be deemed confidential ("Confidential Information") and subject to nondisclosure.
perturbation.explanation: Here, replacing 'Confidential Information' with 'Protected Information' at the beginning of Section 1, while still referencing 'Confidential Information' later in the same section, creates confusion. It's unclear which term applies and if they have the same definition. The continued use of the original term in defining the information makes the contract internally inconsistent. This contradicts the definition section itself
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Protected Information. Unless instructed otherwise in writing, the parties acknowledge that all information, knowledge, or data relating to the business of Client, including but not limited to: costs and financial information; pricing methods or strategies; existing and potential customers; market studies, plans and strategies; trade secrets, know how, ideas, strategies, inventions, materials, drawings, proprietary information, operational information, and any other non-public information, material or data relating to the past, current and/or future business and operations of Client (including any information, summaries or other compilations extracted from such shared information), shared for review, and in all instances marked as confidential, belongs to and is the sole and exclusive property of Client and shall be deemed confidential ("Confidential Information") and subject to nondisclosure. Confidential Information shall not include: (a) information which can be shown to have been in the public domain at the time of disclosure, (b) documented information which was possessed prior to the time of its disclosure hereunder, (c) information that becomes part of the public domain by publication or otherwise not due to any unauthorized act or omission on the part of the parties or party, or (d) information that is given to the parties without obligation of secrecy by a third party, who is free to disclose the information, as of the date of such third party's disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Confidential Information'. The changed text renames 'Confidential Information' to 'Protected Information' at the beginning of the definition, while continuing to define it as 'Confidential Information' in the same section. The contradicted text continues to reference both terms, now with the ambiguity of what each one refers to. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because the contract now uses two terms, 'Protected Information' and 'Confidential Information', interchangeably without properly defining whether they are the same, leading to conflicting obligations as per the Misaligned Terminology definition.

perturbation.original_text: 2. Use of Confidential Information. The parties acknowledge that any use of shared Confidential Information other than for the benefit of Client or the furtherance of a business relationship with Client, would be wrongful and cause irreparable harm to Client. Accordingly, unless otherwise agreed to in writing by Client, Chemsultants agrees to (a) keep all Confidential Information confidential and to not, at any time during or after any transaction where Confidential Information disclosures are made, publish, disclose, or divulge Confidential Information to any person, firm, or corporation other than its Representatives and to cause its Representatives to observe the terms of this Agreement; (b) not use, directly or indirectly, copy, or reproduce any Confidential Information of Client for its own benefit or for the benefit of any person, firm, or corporation other than Client, and in any event only in connection with evaluating or engaging in a transaction or business relationship with Client. Chemsultants hereby agrees it shall be responsible for any breach by it or its Representatives. For the purposes of this Agreement, the term “Representative” shall mean the party’s affiliates, directors, officers, employees, agents, advisors (including, without limitation, financial advisors, legal counsel and accountants) and controlling persons, who are actively and directly participating in any transaction with Client or who otherwise need to know the Confidential Information for the purpose of furthering any transaction with Client, and who agree to be bound by the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Use of Sensitive Data. The parties acknowledge that any use of shared Confidential Information other than for the benefit of Client or the furtherance of a business relationship with Client, would be wrongful and cause irreparable harm to Client. Accordingly, unless otherwise agreed to in writing by Client, Chemsultants agrees to (a) keep all Confidential Information confidential and to not, at any time during or after any transaction where Confidential Information disclosures are made, publish, disclose, or divulge Confidential Information to any person, firm, or corporation other than its Representatives and to cause its Representatives to observe the terms of this Agreement; (b) not use, directly or indirectly, copy, or reproduce any Confidential Information of Client for its own benefit or for the benefit of any person, firm, or corporation other than Client, and in any event only in connection with evaluating or engaging in a transaction or business relationship with Client. Chemsultants hereby agrees it shall be responsible for any breach by it or its Representatives. For the purposes of this Agreement, the term “Representative” shall mean the party’s affiliates, directors, officers, employees, agents, advisors (including, without limitation, financial advisors, legal counsel and accountants) and controlling persons, who are actively and directly participating in any transaction with Client or who otherwise need to know the Sensitive Data for the purpose of furthering any transaction with Client, and who agree to be bound by the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the title of Section 2 to 'Use of Sensitive Data' introduces confusion. The text within the section continues to refer to 'Confidential Information,' but the representatives are now referenced as needing to know 'Sensitive Data' which creates an in-text contradiction because it is not defined if Sensitive Data is related to Confidential information, which makes it unclear whether the obligations under Section 2 apply to information *other* than what is defined in Section 1 as "Confidential Information."
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Use of Sensitive Data. The parties acknowledge that any use of shared Confidential Information other than for the benefit of Client or the furtherance of a business relationship with Client, would be wrongful and cause irreparable harm to Client. Accordingly, unless otherwise agreed to in writing by Client, Chemsultants agrees to (a) keep all Confidential Information confidential and to not, at any time during or after any transaction where Confidential Information disclosures are made, publish, disclose, or divulge Confidential Information to any person, firm, or corporation other than its Representatives and to cause its Representatives to observe the terms of this Agreement; (b) not use, directly or indirectly, copy, or reproduce any Confidential Information of Client for its own benefit or for the benefit of any person, firm, or corporation other than Client, and in any event only in connection with evaluating or engaging in a transaction or business relationship with Client. Chemsultants hereby agrees it shall be responsible for any breach by it or its Representatives. For the purposes of this Agreement, the term “Representative” shall mean the party’s affiliates, directors, officers, employees, agents, advisors (including, without limitation, financial advisors, legal counsel and accountants) and controlling persons, who are actively and directly participating in any transaction with Client or who otherwise need to know the Sensitive Data for the purpose of furthering any transaction with Client, and who agree to be bound by the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the title of Section 2 to "Use of Sensitive Data" while the body of the section, and the definition of 'Representative' within that section, continues to refer to 'Confidential Information' and 'Sensitive Data'. This introduces ambiguity and a potential in-text contradiction because it becomes unclear whether the obligations under Section 2 apply only to 'Confidential Information' as previously defined or also to potentially different 'Sensitive Data.' This misalignment creates a contradiction because it is not defined whether Sensitive Data is related to Confidential information, which makes it unclear whether the obligations under Section 2 apply to information *other* than what is defined in Section 1 as "Confidential Information."

perturbation.original_text: 5. Return of Documents. Upon written request by Client at any time, Chemsultants shall promptly return all Confidential Information, including all copies, reproductions, summaries, analyses or extracts thereof or based thereon in the party’s p o s s e s s i o n o r i n t h e p o s s e s s i o n o f a n y R e p r e s e n t a t i v e o f t h e p a r t y , p r o v i d e d , however, Chemsultants shall be permitted to charge Client for all reasonable costs incurred by Chemsultants for compliance with this Section.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Return of Documents. Upon written request by Client at any time, Chemsultants shall promptly return all Company Data, including all copies, reproductions, summaries, analyses or extracts thereof or based thereon in the party’s p o s s e s s i o n o r i n t h e p o s s e s s i o n o f a n y R e p r e s e n t a t i v e o f t h e p a r t y , p r o v i d e d , however, Chemsultants shall be permitted to charge Client for all reasonable costs incurred by Chemsultants for compliance with this Section.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Company Data' in Section 5, while the agreement still defines and heavily relies on the term 'Confidential Information' creates a direct contradiction. It's no longer clear what Chemsultants is obligated to return. This change directly affects Section 5, creating an obligation which is now ambiguous. The in-text contradiction occurs because the return obligation is not tied to the defined term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Return of Documents. Upon written request by Client at any time, Chemsultants shall promptly return all Company Data, including all copies, reproductions, summaries, analyses or extracts thereof or based thereon in the party’s p o s s e s s i o n o r i n t h e p o s s e s s i o n o f a n y R e p r e s e n t a t i v e o f t h e p a r t y , p r o v i d e d , however, Chemsultants shall be permitted to charge Client for all reasonable costs incurred by Chemsultants for compliance with this Section.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Company Data' in Section 5. The Perturbation Explanation states that this change creates a contradiction because the agreement defines and heavily relies on the term 'Confidential Information', and the return obligation is now tied to 'Company Data'. Therefore, Chemsultants obligation has been altered. The contradicted text is the changed text, meaning that the change introduced the contradiction within the same section. As a result of this change, there is a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6. Remedies. It is agreed that money damages would be an insufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement, and the non-breaching party shall be entitled to injunctive relief, specific performance, and/or any other appropriate equitable remedy for any such breach or threatened breach. Such remedies shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Remedies. It is agreed that money damages would be an insufficient remedy for any breach of this NDA, and the non-breaching party shall be entitled to injunctive relief, specific performance, and/or any other appropriate equitable remedy for any such breach or threatened breach. Such remedies shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'this Agreement' to 'this NDA' creates ambiguity. While the agreement is an NDA, using the abbreviation 'NDA' in the remedies section makes it unclear whether the specified remedies apply to *all* potential breaches, or just those *specifically* related to non-disclosure. Because the initial definition of agreement is 'This Agreement', it introduces a potential loophole where breaches of other aspects of the agreement may not be subject to these remedies. This impacts the entire remedies section, as it weakens the enforceability of the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. Remedies. It is agreed that money damages would be an insufficient remedy for any breach of this NDA, and the non-breaching party shall be entitled to injunctive relief, specific performance, and/or any other appropriate equitable remedy for any such breach or threatened breach. Such remedies shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'this Agreement' to 'this NDA' introduces a potential ambiguity. While the agreement in its entirety may be an NDA, the change raises a valid point and possibility that the remedies section *only* applies to NDA breaches, and not to other breaches of the overall 'Agreement'. The contradicted text and changed text being in the same location further exacerbates the confusion of what the remedies entail for the Agreement as a whole. This impacts the enforceability and creates a direct conflict as to whether or not the remedies apply to the entirety of the contract or a smaller subsection of the contract, which is an in-text contradiction as described in the prompt.

perturbation.original_text: 9. Modification. This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the parties concerning the confidentiality of the Confidential Information and cannot be modified, released, discharged, abandoned, or otherwise terminated in whole or in part unless approved in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. Modification. This Contract contains the entire agreement between the parties concerning the confidentiality of the Confidential Information and cannot be modified, released, discharged, abandoned, or otherwise terminated in whole or in part unless approved in writing.
perturbation.explanation: Substituting 'This Agreement' with 'This Contract' in Section 9 creates a contradiction, especially considering the agreement is consistently referred to as the 'Agreement' throughout the rest of the document. If 'Contract' refers to the entire document, it implies that *all* clauses, even those not directly related to confidentiality, cannot be modified without written approval. The text defining modification of the existing NDA creates an in-text contradiction with the definition in the preamble of 'Agreement' instead of 'Contract'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Modification. This Contract contains the entire agreement between the parties concerning the confidentiality of the Confidential Information and cannot be modified, released, discharged, abandoned, or otherwise terminated in whole or in part unless approved in writing. This Agreement may not be assigned by either party, by operation of law or otherwise, without the prior written consent of the other party, and any attempt to do so shall be void, provided, however, that either party shall be permitted to assign this agreement to any purchaser of all or substantially all of such party’s assets or equity interests, without consent, provided such purchaser agrees to be bound by all terms and conditions contained herein.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'This Agreement' to 'This Contract' in Section 9, as explained, creates a contradiction because the agreement is consistently referred to as 'Agreement' elsewhere. The changed text implies that *all* clauses are subject to modification only with written approval, conflicting with other sections that refer to the modification of the 'Agreement'. The provided 'Contradicted Text' further underscores this conflict, as it continues to reference 'Agreement' in the same section that now uses 'Contract', resulting in a strong in-text contradiction under 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.
